---
title: "Creating components"
description: "How OpenPipeline uses Viash components — the mechanics live in the Viash docs."
order: 20
---

A component is one step of a pipeline: a script plus a `config.vsh.yaml` that
describes its inputs, outputs, dependencies, and container. OpenPipeline builds
every component with [Viash](https://viash.io), so the **how-to for writing a
component is the Viash documentation** — this page only covers the conventions
specific to OpenPipeline.

:::{.callout-tip}
New to Viash? Start with the [Viash guide](https://viash.io/guide/) and the
[config reference](https://viash.io/reference/config/). The mechanics there
(argument groups, `engines`, `runners`, the `## VIASH START` / `## VIASH END`
block, building images) apply as-is to OpenPipeline.
:::

## OpenPipeline conventions

- **One common data format.** Components read and write the same
  [MuData](../concepts/mudata.qmd) object, so any component can slot before or
  after any other. Convert to/from other formats only at the edges.
- **Where it lives.** Components sit in `src/<namespace>/<component>/`, grouped
  into [namespaces](project-structure.qmd#sec-project-structure) by function
  (e.g. `convert`, `filter`, `dimred`). Look for an existing component that does
  something similar before starting a new one.
- **Author info is centralized.** Each contributor has a YAML file in
  `src/authors/`, merged into configs with Viash's `__merge__` directive, so
  author details stay consistent without duplication:

  ```yaml
  authors:
    - __merge__: /src/authors/your_name.yaml
      roles: [ author ]
  ```

- **Resource labels.** Declare CPU/memory (and GPU) needs with labels in the
  component's `nextflow` runner — see [Tune compute resources](../guides/tune-resources.qmd).
- **Base containers.** Start from a reputable, actively-maintained base image and
  install dependencies through a package manager. Common choices (use the current
  tag rather than pinning an old one):
  - [`python`](https://hub.docker.com/_/python) — Python environments
  - [`ubuntu`](https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu) — general Linux and bash scripts
  - [`rocker`](https://rocker-project.org/) images — R
  - [`nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch`](https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nvidia/containers/pytorch) — GPU-accelerated PyTorch in Python

## Build and test locally

While developing, `viash run` and `viash test` build the component on the fly —
no separate build step. See [Running tests](running-tests.qmd). The published,
versioned package is built and hosted by Viash Hub
([packages.viash-hub.com](https://packages.viash-hub.com)), not committed to the
repository.

**Next:** [Creating pipelines →](creating-pipelines.qmd)
